channel.title: Cybersecurity News Aggregator
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity news from top sources
channel.comment: News aggregated from: Cointelegraph - Security, Threatpost, Bleeping Computer, Dark Reading, Blockthreat, CryptoPotato, CryptoSlate, Krebs On Security, The Hacker News, Coindesk, GroupIB Blog, Data Breach Today, CNN.com - Crypto News, DataBreaches.net

item.title: Bitcoin Records Second-Largest Unrealized Loss in History Amid Market Pressure
item.description: The Bitcoin market is suffering, but holders have not surrendered their coins yet, according to Alphractal founder....
item.dc:creator: CryptoPotato

item.title: DOJ seizes CFAKE, SOCFAKE deepfake nude sites under TAKE IT DOWN Act
item.description: The U.S. Department of Justice announced Friday that it has seized the CFAKE.com and SOCFAKE.com websites, which allegedly hosted nonconsensual AI-generated nude images and videos of women, in what ap...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: SimpleHelp bug lets hackers create rogue remote support accounts
item.description: A vulnerability in the SimpleHelp remote management software allows unauthenticated attackers to create privileged technician accounts on servers using the OpenID Connect (OIDC) authentication protoco...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Chinese Hackers Abused Google Workspace Rules to Steal Research and Defense Emails
item.description:
A China-linked espionage group hid inside North American medical, academic, and military research networks for more than a year, quietly stealing sensitive research and defense email.
The way in was ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Copilot 'SearchLeak' Attack Allows 1-Click Data Theft

item.title: LiteLLM Vulnerability Chain Lets Low-Privilege Users Take Over AI Gateway Servers
item.description:
A default low-privilege account on a LiteLLM proxy can climb to full admin and run code on the server by chaining three vulnerabilities, researchers at Obsidian Security disclosed
LiteLLM is a widely...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: One-Click Microsoft 365 Copilot Flaw Could Have Let Attackers Steal Emails, Files, and MFA Codes
item.description:
A single click on a trusted Microsoft link could have let an attacker pull emails, calendar details, and indexed files out of Microsoft 365 Copilot Enterprise Search.
Researchers at Varonis Threat La...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Over 400 Arch Linux AUR Packages Hijacked to Deploy Infostealer and eBPF Rootkit
item.description:
Attackers took over more than 400 packages in the Arch User Repository (AUR) this week and rewrote their build scripts to install a credential stealer on any machine that built them.
The malware is a...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Ukrainian national pleads guilty to role in Conti ransomware operation
item.description: Lawrence Abrams reports: A Ukrainian national extradited from Ireland to the United States last year has pleaded guilty to conspiracy charges tied to the Conti ransomware operation. The U.S. Departmen...
item.dc:creator: DataBreaches.net

item.title: Labcorp reaches $35M settlement over American Medical Collection Agency breach
item.description: Do you remember the horrific American Medical Collection Agency (Retrieval-Masters Creditors Bureau Inc.) breach in 2019? You can refresh your memory by scrolling through the Related posts below this...
item.dc:creator: DataBreaches.net

item.title: Google Sues Chinese Smishing Network Accused of Using Gemini AI in Phishing
item.description: Google on Friday said it's pursuing legal action against a Chinese cybercrime network, accusing it of using its Gemini artificial intelligence (AI) agent to send phishing text messages targeting Ameri...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: China-Linked Hackers Backdoored Linux Login Software to Hide for Nearly a Decade
item.description:
Instead of hiding on the laptops and servers defenders watch most closely, a China-nexus group spent close to a decade hidden inside the Linux login system itself.
Sygnia, which tracks the group as V...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: ShinyHunters Targets Education Sector with Oracle PeopleSoft Exploit
item.description: From Mandiant and Google Threat Intelligence Group, an advisory: Mandiant and Google Threat Intelligence Group (GTIG) have identified an active compromise and extortion campaign attributed to UNC6240 ...
item.dc:creator: DataBreaches.net

item.title: After a Massive Hack, Global Schools Group’s Negotiator Acted “Bizarrely.” It Didn’t End Well for Them.
item.description: The bigger they are, the harder they fail? Global Schools Foundation (GSF) is a Singapore-headquartered, not-for-profit K–12 education organization. With a global network of schools, the foundation st...

item.title: Claude Fable 5 Doesn't Change the Mythos Security Story
item.description: Stay cool: Mythos 5 is an upgrade over Mythos Preview while Fable 5 is Mythos "made safe for general use," Anthropic explains....

item.title: Metaplanet to form securities arm through Siiibo acquisition
item.description: Metaplanet will acquire Siiibo Securities and rename it Metaplanet Securities as it builds out Project Nova, its Bitcoin-centric finance strategy in Japan....
item.dc:creator: Cointelegraph - Security

item.title: Agentjacking Attack Tricks AI Coding Agents Into Running Malicious Code
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have described what they say is a new class of attack that can trick artificial intelligence (AI) coding agents into running arbitrary code on developer machines.
Called Age...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Rethinking MDR as Attackers and Defenders Embrace AI
item.description: For most of the past decade, managed detection and response was the answer to a real problem. Security teams couldn't staff around the clock, couldn't hire enough analysts, and needed someone else to ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: LangGraph Flaw Chain Exposes Self-Hosted AI Agents to Remote Code Execution
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of three now-patched security flaws impacting LangGraph, including a critical vulnerability chain that could result in remote code execution.
LangGrap...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News
